entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop tested for seed transmission of IPCV

entities.name: INDIAN PEANUT CLUMP VIRUS
entities.description: A furovirus that affects peanut and millet crops

entities.name: ELISA
entities.description: An enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay procedure used to test seeds for viral presence

entities.name: DOUBLE ANTIBODY SANDWICH ELISA
entities.description: A specific ELISA format used to detect the IPCV-H isolate

entities.name: IPCV-H
entities.description: A specific isolate of the Indian peanut clump virus from Hyderabad

entities.name: SEED TRANSMISSION
entities.description: The process by which a virus is transmitted from the parent plant to the offspring via the seed

entities.name: GROW-OUT TESTS
entities.description: Tests used to verify the presence of a virus by growing the seeds

entities.name: COTYLEDONS
entities.description: The first leaves produced by the embryo of a seed, found to contain the virus

entities.name: EMBRYO
entities.description: The part of the seed that develops into a plant, contributing to seed transmission

entities.name: TESTAS
entities.description: The protective outer coat of a seed, containing viral antigen

entities.name: VIRAL ANTIGEN
entities.description: A protein or molecule from the virus detected in the seed testas

entities.name: FINGER MILLET
entities.description: A millet crop (Eleusine coracana) showing seed transmission of IPCV-H

entities.name: FOXTAIL MILLET
entities.description: A millet crop (Setaria italica) showing seed transmission of IPCV-H

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A millet crop (Pennisetum glaucum) showing seed transmission of IPCV-H

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A crop in which seed transmission of IPCV was not observed

entities.name: GENOTYPE
entities.description: The genetic makeup of the plant, which influences the frequency of seed transmission
